attack_surface.exposure: Every filesystem touch from action handlers flows through `path.@this.Authorize` â†’ AuthGate. AuthGate's auto-grant fast path is `IsInRoot()` (textual StartsWith of `_absolutePath` against the app root).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Any caller producing a `path` whose `_absolutePath` textually prefix-matches the app root.
mitigations: AuthGate(verb) required by every IO verb on FilePath; PLNG002 at error severity prevents handlers from bypassing path.@this entirely.

v2: FilePath ctor now canonicalizes `_absolutePath` via PathHelper.GetFullPath â€” every code path (Resolve, derivation verbs, scheme registry, implicit stringâ†’path, JsonConverter) inherits the fix for free. `..` segments can no longer survive into `_absolutePath`.
gaps: None reachable. Three deliberate canonicalization skips (empty, non-rooted, `//`-prefixed) verified safe in v2 result.md.

attack_surface.exposure: Combine/WithName/InFolder/Parent verbs on FilePath build new FilePath objects.
mitigations: Derivation produces a Path object only â€” no IO. All IO must go through Operations, which gate.
v2: Each derivation verb passes the combined string through `new @this(...)` â†’ ctor Canonicalize â†’ GetFullPath. `..` introduced via derivation is resolved before the next AuthGate check.

attack_surface.exposure: `path.LoadAssemblyAsync()` is the only `Assembly.LoadFrom` site (grep-verified). Gated by `Verb { Execute }`.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: An actor wanting to load a DLL must have an Execute grant â€” Read grant does NOT cover Execute.
mitigations: Verb.AllowAll() includes Execute, but AllowAll is test-only. Production grants flow through `BuildRequest(actor, verb)` with the narrow requested verb.

attack_surface.exposure: `PathJsonConverter` reads a string off the wire and resolves it via `@this.Resolve(raw, context)` â†’ Scheme.From â†’ file.Resolve â†’ ValidatePath, or via the implicit `string â†’ path` operator when no Context.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Anything inbound on a channel whose payload deserializes to a `Data<path>` field.
mitigations: Context-bound converter routes through Resolve; stub paths (no-Context) explode at Authorize.
v2: Both pathways (Resolve, implicit operator) construct via `new file.@this(raw)` â†’ Canonicalize. Wire `"subdir/../../etc/passwd"` no longer survives into `_absolutePath`.

attack_surface.exposure: `FilePath.Exists` and `FilePath.Size` (sync `[LlmBuilder]` properties) call System.IO.File.Exists / new FileInfo(Absolute).Length without AuthGate.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Template/LLM-builder surfaces. Calling them at runtime gives existence/size for the path the caller has.
gaps: Sync `Exists`/`Size` are info-disclosure side channels under sub-actor probing â€” under PLang's user-sovereign threat model these read user-authored templates only. Standing watch (no change v1â†’v2).

attack_surface.exposure: MarkdownTeaching loader walks a `path.@this` modulesRoot via `ExistsAsync`/`List`/`ReadText` to render per-action LLM teaching files.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: `Modules.MarkdownTeachingRoot` is publicly settable from C#; default derives from `App.OsDirectory`.
mitigations: v2: All disk reach lifted to `path.@this` verb surface (987a5148e). `ResolveMarkdownTeachingRoot()` routes the string override through `path.@this.Resolve(System.Context)` â€” any out-of-root override hits AuthGate at the first verb call.
PLNG002 whole-file exemption for MarkdownTeaching.cs dropped.

attack_surface.exposure: New `PLang/app/Utils/PathHelper.cs` is the single bridge to `System.IO.Path.*` â€” Combine/Join/Get*Name/GetFullPath/IsPathRooted/separator constants. PLNG002 carves out only `Path.*` here, only for this file.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Every other PLang file must route Path.* through PathHelper; new IO members added to PathHelper would still fire PLNG002's File/Directory carve-out (PathHelper.cs is not exempt for that pillar).
mitigations: PathHelper body verified pure name math: no `File.*`, `Directory.*`, `FileInfo`, `FileStream`. `GetTempPath`/`GetTempFileName` explicitly forbidden by the class doc.
PLNG002's two-pronged carve-out (file scope + member kind) means a hostile File.* addition still fires.

findings.vector: IsInRoot()'s auto-grant fast path used a textual `StartsWith` of `_absolutePath` against the app root, with no `..`-segment normalization. When `file.Resolve` combined a relative path containing `..` with a goal-anchored runtimeDir (or goalPath.Parent) inside root, the resulting absolute string textually prefix-matched root â€” IsInRoot returned true â€” AuthGate auto-granted â€” and `System.IO.File.*` resolved the `..` segments at the OS layer and read outside root with no actor prompt.
findings.preconditions: A `path` whose `_absolutePath` contained unresolved `..` segments AND textually prefix-matched `app.AbsolutePath`. Natural source: `file.Resolve` for a relative rawPath when the active Goal has a `LoadedFromPrPath` inside root.
findings.impact: Arbitrary file read (and write/delete/execute by symmetry) outside the actor's permission scope, without prompt or grant lookup. Confirmed via 5s mutation test in v1.
findings.exploit_sketch: v1 reproduced via mutation test; v2 ported to a real regression suite: PLang.Tests/App/Types/PathTests/DotDotTraversalRegressionTests.cs.
findings.proposed_fix: Canonicalize at FilePath ctor (preferred shape (b) from v1 result) â€” fixed in 064724fda.
findings.fix_summary: `file.@this`'s ctor canonicalizes `_absolutePath` via `PathHelper.GetFullPath` before storing. Every construction path inherits the fix. Regression test ported in PathTests/DotDotTraversalRegressionTests.cs covers ctor, Resolve+`..`, and ReadText denial. v2 mutation test (Canonicalize â†’ return absolutePath) confirms all 3 regression tests fail without the fix; revert clean.

findings.vector: `PLang/app/modules/MarkdownTeaching.cs` performed 8 ungated System.IO reaches under a whole-file PLNG002 exemption. Today's caller hardcodes /system/modules, but `Modules.MarkdownTeachingRoot` is publicly settable; if wired from config or wire input the loader silently enumerated and read any directory with no AuthGate.
findings.preconditions: Future C# change to set MarkdownTeachingRoot from untrusted input.
findings.impact: Latent. Whole-file exemption guaranteed the analyzer would never warn about a new System.IO addition.
findings.exploit_sketch: Theoretical â€” required future C# change.
findings.proposed_fix: Lift to path.@this verb surface â€” fixed in 987a5148e.
findings.fix_summary: Every disk touch routes through path verbs (`ExistsAsync`, `Combine`, `List`, `ReadText`). `ResolveMarkdownTeachingRoot` routes the string override through `path.@this.Resolve(System.Context)` so out-of-root overrides hit AuthGate at first verb. Whole-file PLNG002 exemption removed.

findings.vector: PLNG002 whole-file exempted `PLang/app/this.cs`. An inline `#pragma warning disable PLNG002` looked like it scoped to the `OsAbsolutePath` lines, but the analyzer didn't honor source pragmas â€” the actual exemption was whole-file via `IsScannedFile`. The pragma's apparent scope misled readers about what was actually exempted.
findings.preconditions: None today. The only System.IO reach was OsAbsolutePath (pure path arithmetic).
findings.impact: Latent. A future engineer adding any System.IO call anywhere in app/this.cs would silently pass the gate.
findings.proposed_fix: Introduce PathHelper, drop pragma + whole-file exemption â€” fixed in 064724fda.
findings.fix_summary: PathHelper introduced as the single allowed bridge to System.IO.Path.*. OsAbsolutePath now `PathHelper.GetFullPath(PathHelper.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "os"))`. Inline `#pragma` removed. Whole-file exemption for app/this.cs removed from `IsScannedFile`. PLNG002 narrowed to two carve-outs: `Path.*` â†’ only PathHelper.cs; `File/Directory/FileInfo/...` â†’ only app/types/path/**.

summary: v2 verdict: PASS. All three v1 findings closed by 064724fda + 987a5148e. F1 (HIGH path-traversal) fixed by canonicalizing `_absolutePath` at the FilePath ctor â€” every construction path inherits. F1 fix mutation-verified: all 3 regression tests in PathTests/DotDotTraversalRegressionTests.cs fail when Canonicalize is short-circuited. F2 (LOW, latent-Medium MarkdownTeaching ungated IO) fixed by lifting to the path verb surface and routing the publicly-settable root through `path.@this.Resolve(System.Context)`. F3 (LOW, misleading whole-file PLNG002 exemption on app/this.cs) fixed by introducing PathHelper + narrowing PLNG002 to two visible carve-outs. PathHelper contract verified pure name math â€” no File/Directory/FileInfo/Stream forwarders, no IO. PLNG002 has no whole-file exemptions remaining.
